title: FoodGuard AI — Food Health Scanner App

requirements.text: Implement a Splash Screen that displays the FoodGuard AI logo, app name, and a brief tagline, shown briefly on app launch before transitioning to the Home Screen.
acceptanceCriteria: Splash screen is shown on initial app load for approximately 2 seconds
Displays the app logo, name 'FoodGuard AI', and a short tagline
Automatically transitions to the Home Screen after the delay

requirements.text: Implement a Home Screen with a prominent 'Scan Food' button, navigation to the Profile screen, and a brief description of the app's purpose.
acceptanceCriteria: Home screen displays a large, tappable 'Scan Food' button
Navigation link/icon to the Profile screen is accessible from Home
Brief description or tagline is visible below the app name

requirements.text: Implement a Camera Screen that activates the device camera when 'Scan Food' is pressed, allowing the user to capture an image of a food item or packaged food label/barcode. Provide a 'Capture' button and display a viewfinder overlay.
acceptanceCriteria: Camera view is shown using the browser MediaDevices API
A 'Capture' button takes a snapshot of the current frame
A viewfinder/frame overlay guides the user to position the food item
User can cancel and return to the Home Screen

requirements.text: Implement a food analysis simulation on the backend: given a food item name (string input from the frontend after image capture), return a structured result including food name, estimated calories, sugar level (Low/Medium/High), fat level (Low/Medium/High), a health score (0–100), and a status (Safe / Moderate / Harmful). Store a predefined database of at least 20 common foods with realistic nutritional data.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend exposes a query function that accepts a food name string and returns a FoodResult record
FoodResult includes: foodName, calories, sugarLevel, fatLevel, healthScore (0–100), status ('Safe' | 'Moderate' | 'Harmful')
At least 20 common food items are stored with realistic nutritional values
Health score is computed using the scoring rules: high sugar -20, high fat -15, preservatives -10 from a base of 100
Status maps correctly: 80–100 = Safe, 50–79 = Moderate, below 50 = Harmful

requirements.text: Implement a packaged food ingredient scanner on the frontend: after capturing an image of an ingredient label, simulate OCR by displaying a text input where the user can paste/type extracted ingredient text. Parse the text to detect harmful ingredients: high sugar, MSG, artificial colors (e.g., Red 40, Yellow 5), and preservatives (e.g., sodium benzoate, BHA, BHT). Display warning badges for each detected harmful ingredient.

quotes: Scan barcode OR scan ingredients label

acceptanceCriteria: A text area or input field is presented after 'label scan' mode is selected, accepting ingredient text
The system detects at least: high sugar/sugar variants, MSG/monosodium glutamate, artificial color names (Red 40, Yellow 5, Blue 1, etc.), and common preservatives (sodium benzoate, BHA, BHT, TBHQ)
Each detected harmful ingredient is shown as a colored warning badge
A summary warning message is shown if any harmful ingredient is detected
Backend stores the list of harmful ingredient keywords used for detection

requirements.text: Implement a Result Screen that displays the full health analysis after a scan: food name, calories, sugar level, fat level, a visual circular health score gauge (0–100), color-coded status badge (Green = Safe, Yellow = Moderate, Red = Harmful), and a list of any detected harmful ingredients with warning icons.
acceptanceCriteria: Result screen renders after a scan is completed
Displays food name, estimated calories, sugar level, and fat level
A circular gauge or progress arc displays the health score (0–100)
Status badge is green for Safe (80–100), yellow for Moderate (50–79), red for Harmful (<50)
Harmful ingredient warnings are listed with warning icons when present
A 'Scan Again' button returns the user to the Camera Screen

requirements.text: Implement a Profile Screen where users can enter and save their personal health information: age, weight, and one or more health conditions (options include: Diabetes, High Blood Pressure, High Cholesterol, Heart Disease, Obesity, None). Store profile data in the backend per session.

quotes: User enters: Age, Weight, Health condition (Diabetes, BP, etc.)

acceptanceCriteria: Profile screen has input fields for age (number), weight (number, kg), and a multi-select or checkbox list of health conditions
A 'Save Profile' button persists the data to the backend
Saved profile data is loaded and pre-filled when the user revisits the Profile screen
Backend stores the user profile with age, weight, and conditions array

requirements.text: Implement personalized health warnings on the Result Screen based on the saved user profile: after computing the food's health data, cross-reference with the user's health conditions and display condition-specific warnings (e.g., 'Not recommended for diabetic users' if the food has high sugar and the user has Diabetes; 'High fat content — caution for heart disease' if fat is high and the user has Heart Disease).

quotes: AI gives personalized warning: 'Not recommended for diabetic users.'

acceptanceCriteria: Backend exposes a function that accepts a food result and a user profile and returns a list of personalized warning strings
At least 4 condition-food interactions are handled: Diabetes + high sugar, High Blood Pressure + high sodium/preservatives, Heart Disease + high fat, Obesity + high calories
Personalized warnings are displayed as a distinct section on the Result Screen with an alert icon
No warnings are shown if the user has no saved health conditions or if the food poses no relevant risk

requirements.text: Apply a cohesive visual design across all screens: use a clean, health-focused aesthetic with a deep green and white primary palette, bold modern typography, card-based layouts, and smooth page transitions. The design should feel medical-grade yet approachable, with clear iconography and ample whitespace.
acceptanceCriteria: All screens share a consistent color palette (deep green primary, white backgrounds, red/yellow/green for status indicators)
Typography is consistent using a single modern sans-serif font scale
Cards are used to group content sections on Result and Profile screens
Navigation between screens uses smooth CSS transitions
The app is responsive and optimized for a mobile viewport (max-width ~430px)

constraints: No real AI or ML model integration — food identification and OCR must be simulated using predefined data and keyword matching
No external camera SDKs — use the browser's native MediaDevices API for camera access
No barcode scanning library integration — barcode mode can show a UI placeholder or manual entry fallback
All data must be stored in the Motoko backend; no external databases
App must be optimized for mobile viewport widths
nonGoals: Real-time AI image recognition or cloud ML inference
Actual OCR processing of captured images
Nutritional database API integrations (USDA, Open Food Facts, etc.)
Calorie tracking history or meal logging over time

required: FoodGuard AI app logo: a stylized shield icon with a green leaf or checkmark inside, clean and modern on a white background (foodguard-logo.dim_256x256.png)
Splash screen background: soft gradient from deep green to light mint, abstract healthy food silhouettes subtly blended in (splash-bg.dim_1080x1920.png)
Home screen hero illustration: a flat-style illustration of a hand holding a smartphone scanning a bowl of colorful fresh food (home-hero.dim_800x600.png)
